informal: Prove that a group of order $p^2$, $p$ a prime, has a normal subgroup of order $p$.
reasoning: The informal statement claims that any group of order p^2 (p prime) has a normal subgroup of order p. The Lean signature takes a finite group G with |G| = p^2 and p prime, and concludes there exists a subgroup N with |N| = p that is normal. The Fintype assumption on G is needed in Lean to talk about cardinality, matching the implicit finiteness of the informal statement (order p^2 implies finite). The existential conclusion exactly captures "has a normal subgroup of order p". Quantifier structure, hypotheses, and conclusion all align.
